context: The agent reads docs/vision.md and writes docs/requirements.md containing functional requirements (user stories), non-functional requirements (measurable quality attributes), and constraints in separate Markdown tables. The grader checks format, ID conventions, measurability, structural separation, and table completeness.

checklist.name: Correct output path
checklist.description: The requirements catalog is written to docs/requirements.md (not any other path)

checklist.name: FR user story format
checklist.description: Every functional requirement row's description follows the exact format 'As a [role], I want [goal] so that [benefit]' — no plain-sentence FRs present

checklist.name: NFRs are measurable
checklist.description: Every non-functional requirement contains a numeric threshold or measurable value (e.g. '< 2 seconds', '99.9%', '10,000 concurrent users') — no vague terms like 'fast', 'reliable', or 'secure' used alone as the NFR description

checklist.name: Separate tables per type
checklist.description: Functional requirements, non-functional requirements, and constraints each appear in their own dedicated table — FR, NFR, and C rows are NOT mixed into a single combined table

checklist.name: FR ID prefix
checklist.description: All functional requirement IDs use the FR-NNN prefix (e.g. FR-001, FR-002)

checklist.name: NFR ID prefix
checklist.description: All non-functional requirement IDs use the NFR-NNN prefix (e.g. NFR-001, NFR-002)

checklist.name: Constraint ID prefix
checklist.description: All constraint IDs use the C-NNN prefix (e.g. C-001, C-002)

checklist.name: No duplicate IDs
checklist.description: No ID value appears more than once across the entire document (checked across all three tables)

checklist.name: Status column filled
checklist.description: Every row in every table has a non-empty Status column value — no blank Status cells

checklist.name: NFR category column
checklist.description: The NFR table includes a Category column and every NFR row has a non-empty category value (e.g. Performance, Scalability, Availability, Security, Accessibility)

checklist.name: Singular requirements
checklist.description: Each table row contains exactly one requirement — no row combines multiple distinct requirements with 'and' connecting separate goals
